result:
## Verification Report: 0152-add-cli-defaults-to-settings

| # | Criterion | Result | Evidence |
|---|-----------|--------|----------|
| 1 | `openstation show <task>` opens in vim when `defaults.show.vim` is `true` and run by a human | ✅ PASS | `cli.py:342-345` — `_apply_cli_defaults` runs only when `CLAUDECODE` is unset; `_apply_cli_defaults` sets `vim=True` on unset flags. Integration test `test_show_applies_defaults_for_human` confirms. |
| 2 | `openstation show <task>` ignores defaults when `CLAUDECODE` is set | ✅ PASS | `cli.py:343` — `if not os.environ.get("CLAUDECODE")` guards the entire defaults block. Integration test `test_show_ignores_defaults_when_claudecode_set` confirms. |
| 3 | Explicit flags still work as expected | ✅ PASS | `_explicit_flags()` scans argv for `--flag` and `-f` tokens; `_apply_cli_defaults` skips any flag in the explicit set. Mutually exclusive group safety also prevents conflicts. Tests: `test_does_not_override_explicit_flag`, `test_does_not_apply_when_explicit`, `test_explicit_json_overrides_default_vim`. |
| 4 | `docs/settings.md` documents the `defaults` key and scoping | ✅ PASS | `docs/settings.md` has full `## defaults` section with schema, command key conventions, "Human-Only" scoping subsection, override precedence rules, examples, and architecture/data-flow documentation. |

**All 4 verification items pass.** Shall I proceed to mark the task as done?

tool_input.description: Run CLI defaults tests from project root
Run CLI defaults tests from project root
